title: TGaceAllStories
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from TGaceAllStories, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: More companies than usual are beating Wall Street’s expectations. Why that hasn’t really helped investors.
items.description: Investors will get a better read on the health of consumers as Walmart reports its first quarterly results under its new CEO on Thursday.

items.title: Can you legally refuse to pay back a cash advance? Yes — but there’s a catch.
items.description: Apps offering quick money are surprisingly fine when borrowers use this loophole.

items.title: China is hacking the tariff system by investing in U.S. firms — and American taxpayers foot the bill
items.description: The U.S. Treasury can and should prevent Chinese-linked firms from accessing U.S. tech subsidies.

items.title: ‘I’m a loner’: I’m 62 with a multimillion-dollar portfolio and no heirs. Should I find a wife for my twilight years?
items.description: “I have never been married, have no siblings, and both my parents have passed away.”

items.title: Americans now have much more money in IRAs than 401(k)s. Why that leaves workers more vulnerable.
items.description: With fewer protections and guardrails, we have a much less effective system.

items.title: Netflix and Amazon are among the most oversold stocks on Wall Street
items.description: Several big-name stocks got caught in this week's market sell-off, leaving some at levels that investors could take advantage of to reap future gains.

items.title: Berkshire CEO Abel praises Kraft Heinz for turnaround on planned split
items.description: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO likes the surprise course reversal announced this week by the new CEO of Kraft Heinz.

items.title: Goldman Sachs says these five stocks, including Nvidia, are too attractive to ignore
items.description: Goldman Sachs named a slew of stocks that are attractive at current levels.

items.title: This sector is having its best start since at least 2000. Wall Street likes these dividend payers
items.description: Utilities are on a sharp upswing to begin the year. A couple of dividend-paying stocks are standing out from the pack.

items.title: Josh Brown buys CBRE after AI disruption fears drive steep sell-off
items.description: Shares of CBRE plunged 16% this week on fears that artificial intelligence could weigh on office real estate demand.
